title: Rick Veitch

'''Rick Veitch''' (born [[May 7]], [[1950s#1951|1951]]) is a comic book writer and illustrator who lettered a few issues of the [[Star Wars (1977)|''Marvel Star Wars'']] comic series.

Veitch's career began in the early 1980s when he illustrated a number of fantasy titles for [[Marvel Comics]]' [[Wikipedia:Epic Comics|Epic]] line of comics. He then worked with writer [[Alan Moore]] on [[Wikipedia:Swamp Thing|''Swamp Thing'']] for [[Wikipedia:DC Comics|DC Comics]], and Veitch took over as series writer when Moore left but he would soon quit because of DC's refusal to publish a controversial story which included [[Wikipedia:Jesus Christ|Jesus Christ]]. After leaving DC he began working on alternative press comics like [[Wikipedia:Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles|''Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles'']] and his own superhero parody called [[Wikipedia:Bratpack (comics)|''Bratpack'']]. In 1990s he started an online comic convention website called [http://www.comicon.com comicon.com] where various comic creators and fans can meet in an online format. His brother [[Tom Veitch]] was a comic writer who wrote the ''[[Star Wars: Dark Empire]]'' series as well as the first stories in the [[Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi (comic series)|''Tales of the Jedi'']] series.
